pattern: Allowing up to double bookings means rejecting only intervals that would create a region covered three times, which a running map of overlap-region deltas captures cleanly.
hints: A request is fine as long as no point inside it would be covered by three events at once.
Track not just the events but also the regions that are already double-booked; a new event is rejected if it overlaps any double-booked region.

On each request, compute its intersection with every existing event to find new double-booked regions; if it intersects an already-double-booked region the request is rejected, otherwise commit both the event and the freshly created double regions.

edge_inputs.description: statement example: third overlapping event is rejected, later non-triple events accepted
two overlaps allowed, the third stacked over the same point rejected
shared endpoints never count as overlap so triples of touching intervals are fine
a triple booking only on a sub-region is still rejected
events that double-book disjoint regions stay acceptable

stress.description: many random bookings over a tight coordinate space so triple-booking attempts are common
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    ops = ['MyCalendarTwo']
    args = [[]]
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        s = rng.randint(0, 40)
        e = s + rng.randint(1, 15)
        ops.append('book'); args.append([s, e])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: narrow range guarantees frequent overlaps that push toward triples
